owner_signoff.evidence: Owner's current-turn instruction explicitly approved problem resolution and execution within the enumerated GitHub write limits.
owner_signoff.approved_channel: GitHub issue, existing branch push, pull request, and merge
owner_signoff.excluded: deployment, production server changes, browser/portal execution, login, candidate search/registration, and outreach

red_reproduction.method: Created a safe temporary git-archive copy of the base commit, copied only the new focused test into it, and did not reset or checkout the user worktree.
red_reproduction.command: tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d /tmp/valuehire-search-machines-2node-red.XXXXXX)" && git archive HEAD | tar -x -C "$tmp_dir" && cp tests/test_search_machine_config.py "$tmp_dir/tests/test_search_machine_config.py" && cd "$tmp_dir" && PYTHONSAFEPATH=1 PYTHONPATH=. python -m pytest tests/test_search_machine_config.py -q
red_reproduction.output: Collection error: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tools.multi_position_sourcing.search_machine'

generator.claim: Only VH-SM-001 and VH-SM-002 can start search workers; their ports and canonical OS-correct profile paths cannot collide; loop and healthcheck fail closed on missing or invalid identity; portal launcher consumes an allowlisted non-secret machine env while preserving the exact legacy Mac login profiles.

v1.author: claude -p adversarial review
v1.notes: Read-only review produced no output for 120 seconds and ended with Execution error; no result was trusted.

v2.author: Codex Rescue independent read-only review
counterexamples_reproduced: missing, blank, padded, unknown, and inactive machine IDs
duplicate ports and profiles, including case-only aliases
Windows/Mac profile syntax mixed in either direction
Windows/Mac dot-dot, duplicate-separator, and trailing-separator aliases
Windows trailing-dot and trailing-space component aliases
portal launcher machine-env wiring and exact legacy Mac profiles
secret sentinel output and existing fail-soft daemon regressions
v2.notes: All 11 path attacks were rejected; no practical remaining acceptance/security bypass was found.

failed_attempts: Baseline focused test failed collection as intended because the registry module did not exist.
Initial collision test expected a duplicate error but OS-mixing validation rejected earlier; the fixture was corrected to a true same-OS collision.
Independent review found prefix-suffix OS mixing and macOS case aliases; code and tests were hardened.
Independent re-review found dot-dot and separator aliases; canonical path comparison and relative-segment rejection were added.
Final re-review found Windows trailing-dot/space aliases; Win32-incompatible components were rejected.
First full verify output transport lost its exit code; verify was rerun and later rerun again after final changes with exit 0.

scope.limit: runtime implementation remains at or below 5 files and 300 added lines; tests and evidence artifacts are separate
